items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Connie Hawkins. NYC legend got caught up in mostly BS scandal that shortened his career. Played in EBL & was MVP of early ABA (67-69). Did well for PHO from 69-72 but declined after. Finished up w/LA & ATL thru 76. Passed away in 2017. RIP to this HOFer.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Bonus Hollander POTD Connie Hawkins. NYC legend got caught up in mostly BS scandal that shortened his career. Played in EBL & was MVP of early ABA (67-69). Did well for PHO from 69-72 but declined after. Finished up w/LA & ATL thru 76. Passed away in 2017. RIP to this HOFer.

items.title: One thing I always think about with the rigged crowd: If you actually believe in good faith that the 2020 U.S. Election was rigged, then logically you should be calling out the results of Senate elections in Idaho, Iowa, Kansas, Louisana and on and on, no?
items.author: Richard Deitsch
items.body_excerpt:
One thing I always think about with the rigged crowd: If you actually believe in good faith that the 2020 U.S. Election was rigged, then logically you should be calling out the results of Senate elections in Idaho, Iowa, Kansas, Louisana and on and on, no? 

But stunningly I never see this.

items.title: NEW YORK — Tyrese Haliburton asked LeBron James if he had a Decision to announce to everyone at Fanatics Fest during the live taping of the Mind the Game pod.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
NEW YORK — Tyrese Haliburton asked LeBron James if he had a Decision to announce to everyone at Fanatics Fest during the live taping of the Mind the Game pod.

LeBron: "We just talked about this in the back."

Haliburton: "OK, OK, I'll leave it alone."

And the podcast taping moved on.

items.title: 1) i've found that trying to reply to as many questions as possible and trying to moderate podcast comments is often fruitful and i really appreciate thoughtful discourse
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
1) i've found that trying to reply to as many questions as possible and trying to moderate podcast comments is often fruitful and i really appreciate thoughtful discourse

2) replying to questions and moderating comments can truly be soul-destroying in some cases

tough!
